perturbation.original_text: Limitations . The obligations of the Recipient specified in Section 2 above shall not apply with respect to Confidential Information to the extent that such Confidential Information: (a) is or becomes generally known to the public without any act or omission on the part of the Recipient or its Representatives; (b) is in th e Recipient’s lawful possession at the time of disclosure by the Disclosing Party; (c) is or becomes known to the Recipient t hrough disclosure by an unaffiliated third party (except where such third party is known by the Recipient to be disclosing such inf ormation in breach of obligations of confidence); or (d) is independently developed by or for the Recipient by persons who have had no access to or been informed of the existence or substance of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Limitations . The obligations of the Recipient specified in Section 2 above shall not apply with respect to Confidential Information to the extent that such Confidential Information: (a) is or becomes generally known to the public without any act or omission on the part of the Recipient or its Representatives; (b) is in th e Recipient’s lawful possession at the time of disclosure by the Disclosing Party; (c) is or becomes known to the Recipient t hrough disclosure by an unaffiliated third party; or (d) is independently developed by or for the Recipient by persons who have had no access to or been informed of the existence or substance of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'except where such third party is known by the Recipient to be disclosing such information in breach of obligations of confidence' weakens the confidentiality protection. If the Recipient receives confidential info from a third party *knowing* they breached confidentiality, they are now allowed to use it per the modified clause, contradicting the spirit of the NDA to protect Disclosing Party's information. It is now contradicting the Section 2, about Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non- Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to videotape, photograph or otherwise record any Confidential Information. The Recipient shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third parties other than to its affiliates, and its and their respective officers, directors, employees, consultants or professional adviser s (collectively, “ Representatives ”) who have a need to know the Confidential Information for use in evaluating or pursuing a potential business relationship with Disclosing Party or its affiliates (“ Permitted Use. ”) Recipient will, and will cause its Representatives to (ii) use the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party for no purpose other than the Permitted Use. Recipient shall remain liable for any fai lure by its Representatives to comply with the terms of this Agreement. “Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed by Disclosing Party to Recipient, either directly or indirectly in writing, orally , visually or by inspection of tangible objects in connection with the Meeting and related discussions or the Permitted Use (including, without limitation, research, product plans, products, services, equipment, customers, markets, software, inventi ons, discoveries, ideas, processes, designs, drawings, hardware, formulations, specifications, product configuration information, product components, marketing and finance documents, prototypes, samples, data sets, and equipment), whether or not designated as “confidential” at the time of disc losure.
perturbation.justification: The original text included an exception where the Recipient couldn't use confidential information if they knew it was disclosed by a third party in breach of confidentiality. The changed text removes this exception. Now, the Recipient *can* use confidential information received from a third party, even if they know the third party breached an obligation of confidence. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (Section 2), which broadly prohibits the Recipient from disclosing or using Confidential Information, regardless of the source, and is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: It shall not be a breach of this Agreement for the Recipient to disclose Confidential Information if required to do so under law or in a judicial, arbitral, or governmental proceeding or investigation, provided , (i) the Disclosing Party has been given reasonable prior notice to allow i t the option to take actions to protect its interest and Recipient shall cooperate with any reasonable requests of the Disclosing Party in connect ion thereof, including any protective orders or other safeguards sought by the Disclosing Party ; and (ii) the Recipient only discloses that portion of the Confidential Information (with a full copy to the Disclosing Party) required to be disclosed and shall preserve the confidentiality of all other Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: It shall not be a breach of this Agreement for the Recipient to disclose Confidential Information if required to do so under law or in a judicial, arbitral, or governmental proceeding or investigation, provided , (i) the Disclosing Party has been given reasonable prior notice to allow i t the option to take actions to protect its interest and Recipient shall cooperate with any reasonable requests of the Disclosing Party in connect ion thereof, including any protective orders or other safeguards sought by the Disclosing Party ; and (ii) the Recipient only discloses that portion of the Confidential Information required to be disclosed .
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'with a full copy to the Disclosing Party and shall preserve the confidentiality of all other Confidential Information' creates a contradiction. While disclosure is allowed under legal compulsion, removing the requirement to provide a copy and maintain confidentiality for other parts undermines the Disclosing Party's ability to monitor what was disclosed and to what extent, potentially violating the NDA's core principle of confidentiality. It now contradicts Section 4, Ownership of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership of Confidential Informati on. All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party and nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to grant the Recipient any license, right, title, or interest in or to the Confidential Inform ation. The Recipient acquires no intellectual property license or rights under this Agreement except the limited right to review such Confidential Information. Neither this Agreement nor the disclosure of any Confidential Information hereunder shall result in any obligation on the part of either party to enter into any further agreement with the other or to require Disclosing Party to disclose any particular or further Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement creates or shall be deemed to create any employment, joint venture, or agency relationship between the p arties.
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the requirement for the Recipient to provide a full copy of disclosed Confidential Information to the Disclosing Party and to preserve the confidentiality of all other Confidential Information. This contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that 'All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party' because the Disclosing Party's ability to maintain ownership and control over its Confidential Information is undermined if the Recipient is not obligated to provide a full copy of what was disclosed and maintain the confidentiality of the remaining information. This also falls under the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction, as essential language was removed.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient acknowledges that its breach of this Agreement may cause irreparable damage to Disclosing Party and hereby agrees that, in addition to any other relief available at law or in equity, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient acknowledges that its breach of this Agreement may cause irreparable damage to Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the part 'and hereby agrees that, in addition to any other relief available at law or in equity, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief under this Agreement.' weakens the enforcement mechanism. Although other legal remedies still exist, removing the explicit right to seek injunctive relief makes it less clear if the Disclosing Party can immediately stop a breach. Since Section 2 outlines very strictly how the information needs to be kept, by removing this, is creating in-text contradiction in Section 2, which mentions: Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non- Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to videotape, photograph or otherwise record any Confidential Information. The Recipient shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third parties other than to its affiliates, and its and their respective officers, directors, employees, consultants or professional adviser s (collectively, “ Representatives ”) who have a need to know the Confidential Information for use in evaluating or pursuing a potential business relationship with Disclosing Party or its affiliates (“ Permitted Use. ”) Recipient will, and will cause its Representatives to (ii) use the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party for no purpose other than the Permitted Use. Recipient shall remain liable for any fai lure by its Representatives to comply with the terms of this Agreement. “Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed by Disclosing Party to Recipient, either directly or indirectly in writing, orally , visually or by inspection of tangible objects in connection with the Meeting and related discussions or the Permitted Use (including, without limitation, research, product plans, products, services, equipment, customers, markets, software, inventi ons, discoveries, ideas, processes, designs, drawings, hardware, formulations, specifications, product configuration information, product components, marketing and finance documents, prototypes, samples, data sets, and equipment), whether or not designated as “confidential” at the time of disc losure.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that the Disclosing Party could seek injunctive relief, while the changed text removes this explicit right. The explanation indicates this weakens the enforcement mechanism, particularly in light of Section 2 (the Contradicted Text) which outlines strict requirements for keeping information confidential. By removing the explicit right to seek injunctive relief, the changed text creates a contradiction because it makes the enforcement of the confidentiality obligations in Section 2 less certain, creating a strong in-text contradiction as it makes immediate recourse for breaches less clear.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings , whether written or oral, relating to the subject matter of this Agreement. The parties agree that any representation, warranty or condition, written or otherwise, not expressly contained in this Agreement or in an authorized written amendment thereto shall not be enforceable by any party .
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements and understandings , whether written or oral, relating to the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'The parties agree that any representation, warranty or condition, written or otherwise, not expressly contained in this Agreement or in an authorized written amendment thereto shall not be enforceable by any party' removes a layer of protection for the Disclosing Party. If that part is not there, then there may be oral representation not being enforceable. This now directly affects Section 2, Non-disclosure of confidential information, since if the Non-disclosure is not contained in this agreement, it will not be enforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non- Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to videotape, photograph or otherwise record any Confidential Information. The Recipient shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third parties other than to its affiliates, and its and their respective officers, directors, employees, consultants or professional adviser s (collectively, “ Representatives ”) who have a need to know the Confidential Information for use in evaluating or pursuing a potential business relationship with Disclosing Party or its affiliates (“ Permitted Use. ”) Recipient will, and will cause its Representatives to (ii) use the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party for no purpose other than the Permitted Use. Recipient shall remain liable for any fai lure by its Representatives to comply with the terms of this Agreement. “Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed by Disclosing Party to Recipient, either directly or indirectly in writing, orally , visually or by inspection of tangible objects in connection with the Meeting and related discussions or the Permitted Use (including, without limitation, research, product plans, products, services, equipment, customers, markets, software, inventi ons, discoveries, ideas, processes, designs, drawings, hardware, formulations, specifications, product configuration information, product components, marketing and finance documents, prototypes, samples, data sets, and equipment), whether or not designated as “confidential” at the time of disc losure.
perturbation.justification: The original text contained a clause stating that any representation, warranty, or condition not expressly contained in the agreement is not enforceable. This protected the Disclosing Party. The changed text removes this clause. As the perturbation explanation states, this creates a situation where oral representations might not be enforceable, and the removal of this protection directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which discusses the Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. Specifically, the Confidential Information definition states that it includes information disclosed 'orally'. If the oral information is not part of the written agreement, it may not be enforceable based on the Changed Text, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The confidentiality obligations c ontained in this Agreement expire and are of no further force or effect five (5) years from last date that Confidential Information was disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The confidentiality obligations c ontained in this Agreement expire and are of no further force or effect five (5) years from the date of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'last date that Confidential Information was disclosed under this Agreement' to 'the date of this agreement' limits the duration of confidentiality, even if information is disclosed later. This potentially contradicts the definition of Confidential Information in Section 2, as that would seem to cover information disclosed at any point. There now is an in-text contradiction in the document in Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non- Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to videotape, photograph or otherwise record any Confidential Information. The Recipient shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third parties other than to its affiliates, and its and their respective officers, directors, employees, consultants or professional adviser s (collectively, “ Representatives ”) who have a need to know the Confidential Information for use in evaluating or pursuing a potential business relationship with Disclosing Party or its affiliates (“ Permitted Use. ”) Recipient will, and will cause its Representatives to (ii) use the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party for no purpose other than the Permitted Use. Recipient shall remain liable for any fai lure by its Representatives to comply with the terms of this Agreement. “Confidential Information” means any non-public information disclosed by Disclosing Party to Recipient, either directly or indirectly in writing, orally , visually or by inspection of tangible objects in connection with the Meeting and related discussions or the Permitted Use (including, without limitation, research, product plans, products, services, equipment, customers, markets, software, inventi ons, discoveries, ideas, processes, designs, drawings, hardware, formulations, specifications, product configuration information, product components, marketing and finance documents, prototypes, samples, data sets, and equipment), whether or not designated as “confidential” at the time of disc losure.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined the confidentiality period as five years from the *last* date of disclosure. The changed text shortens this to five years from the agreement date, regardless of when information is disclosed. This directly contradicts the 'Confidential Information' definition in the Contradicted Text, which covers information disclosed at any point, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. The change limits the scope of confidentiality promised in the definition.
